signals.headline: South Korea's annual inflation rose to 2
signals.raw:
South Korea's annual inflation rose to 2.2% in March 2026, up from 2.0% in prior months but below 2.4% forecasts—no major breaking announcement today impacts prediction markets.[1]**  
This March figure, the most recent confirmed data, signals contained price pressures amid Middle East-driven energy volatility, aligning with the Bank of Korea's 2.2% yearly outlook after holding rates at 2.5% for a

signals.headline: | NO | LOW confidence**—No new resolutions, announcements, or confirmations today (April 17, 2026); latest BoK rate hold
signals.raw: | NO | LOW confidence**—No new resolutions, announcements, or confirmations today (April 17, 2026); latest BoK rate hold was April 10, and March CPI lacks intraday updates to shift markets.[3][1]
